notes: Coordinates are map-anchor coordinates for a customary city, town, island, or ceremonial area associated with the festival.
Many festivals are mobile, multi-site, lunar, or diaspora-observed; anchors are intentionally stable for mapping, not exact route geometry.
Fields are intended as a seed dataset for application use and later editorial cleanup.

fields.name: Primary English display name
fields.country_or_countries: One or more countries or broader observing regions
fields.city_or_locality: Anchor place name for map pinning
fields.date_notes: Season/date notes; often variable for lunar festivals
fields.heritage_recognized: Boolean indicating likely UNESCO or similar heritage linkage/recognition, used as seed-level flag only

entries.name: Lunar New Year
entries.date_notes: Late January to February; date varies
entries.summary: Major new year festival celebrated across the Sinosphere and global diaspora communities.

entries.name: Lantern Festival
entries.summary: Lantern-lighting festival marking the close of Lunar New Year celebrations.

entries.name: Qingming Festival
entries.summary: Festival centered on ancestor remembrance and gravesite visits.

entries.name: Dragon Boat Festival
entries.summary: Boat-racing and food traditions linked to seasonal rites and historical memory.

entries.name: Mid-Autumn Festival
entries.summary: Moon-viewing and family reunion festival associated with autumn harvest.

entries.name: Double Ninth Festival
entries.summary: Festival associated with mountain climbing, ancestor respect, and seasonal observance.

entries.name: Obon
entries.summary: Japanese festival honoring ancestors with dances, lanterns, and homecoming traditions.

entries.name: Gion Matsuri
entries.summary: Kyoto's major float festival with long historical roots and elaborate processions.

entries.name: Nebuta Matsuri
entries.summary: Large illuminated float festival in Aomori with dance and taiko performances.

entries.name: Kanda Matsuri
entries.summary: One of Tokyo's major Shinto festivals featuring mikoshi processions.

entries.name: Awa Odori
entries.summary: Major Japanese dance festival held during the Obon season.

entries.name: Setsubun
entries.summary: Seasonal rite marking the start of spring with bean-throwing rituals.

entries.name: Chuseok
entries.summary: Major Korean harvest and ancestor festival centered on family gatherings.

entries.name: Dano
entries.summary: Traditional Korean seasonal festival best known through the Gangneung Danoje celebration.

entries.name: Boryeong Mud Festival
entries.summary: Popular modern Korean festival built around mud-based events and performances.

entries.name: Tết Nguyên Đán
entries.date_notes: Late January to February; date varies
entries.summary: Vietnam's most important annual festival centered on family, ancestors, and renewal.

entries.name: Mid-Autumn Festival (Vietnam)

entries.name: Songkran

entries.name: Loi Krathong
entries.summary: Festival of floating offerings on water as an act of reverence and symbolic release.

entries.name: Thingyan
entries.summary: Myanmar's water festival marking the traditional new year.

entries.name: Pchum Ben

entries.name: Bon Om Touk

entries.name: Ati-Atihan

entries.name: Boun Pi Mai
